sq SqueezyAgent
How it works Docs Benchmarks
GitHub
draft docs / pre-v0

Cost receipts

Receipts make context spend visible: what was read, what was sent, what changed, and what was avoided.

Overview Install pre-v0 Configuration draft Code structure pre-v0 Cost receipts draft Permissions draft Troubleshooting pre-v0
draft

What a receipt should track

Code slices

Path, content hash, byte range, line range, freshness, and reason.

Tool output

Bytes scanned, preview size, spill handle, and model-facing tokens.

Repeated reads

Unchanged ranges should return compact receipt references.

Benchmarks

Receipts become the evidence behind any public savings claim.

Implemented foundation

Current fallback tools already return cost hints, output caps, sha256 receipts, spill handles for large outputs, and aggregate result budgets. Receipt-backed read stubs and structure-aware navigation are later structural savings.

Concrete tool behaviors

Search modes

`grep` can return content, files-with-matches, or counts depending on how much context the model actually needs.

Spill handles

Large reads and tool outputs become compact previews plus local handles for exact follow-up ranges.

Aggregate budget

Parallel tool results are packed against one model-facing budget for the whole round.

Stable tool surface

Tool schemas are sorted deterministically to keep provider prompt/cache prefixes stable.

sq SqueezyAgent

A local static engine helps the agent understand code structure before wasting LLM tokens on search.

Docs Benchmarks Contact GitHub
